question: For the quarterly data platform, which interpretation best reflects what can (and cannot) be inferred from the 100% stacked bar chart?

options.A: Q4 has the highest number of complaints received in absolute terms because it has the largest complaint share.
options.B: Q3 has more online searches than complaints in that quarter.
options.C: The combined height of the bars shows that total platform activity increases from Q1 to Q4.
options.D: All three metrics have the same absolute total each quarter because every bar reaches 100%.

explanation: 100% stacking normalizes unrelated categories to 100% within each quarter. Valid inferences include within-quarter ranking (e.g., one metric > another in every quarter). Invalid inferences include cross-quarter total comparisons or combining unrelated categories.
